understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the currently selected piece (and possibly other selectable pieces) around the grid to reach the goal tile(s) marked with '+', within the available step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempt to shift the selected piece by one grid cell, which corresponds to 3 pixels in the coordinate system (e.g., [9,21] -> [12,21]). If an obstacle is in the way, movement may fail (as when moving right from [15,21] did not change position). Some adjacent objects are pushable blocks that can be pushed if directly next to the selected piece in the move direction, causing both to shift; walls (#) generally block movement but can have level-specific behavior that must be inferred by trying moves. Levels can include multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with the exact pixel coordinate of another selectable switches control to it (e.g., CLICK [33,21] changed the selected piece), after which movement applies to that new selection.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall on the right is truly blocking or has special behavior.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall on the right is truly blocking or has special behavior.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | Even though a wall is adjacent, wall boundaries can behave unexpectedly; try moving right toward the goal area to test if it’s passable or triggers a special interaction.
history.reasoning: Even though a wall is adjacent, wall boundaries can behave unexpectedly; try moving right toward the goal area to test if it’s passable or triggers a special interaction.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether moving right will push the adjacent selectable or reveal special wall behavior toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Test whether moving right will push the adjacent selectable or reveal special wall behavior toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | Current selectable is fully boxed in by walls; switch control to the other selectable at [33, 21] to attempt reaching the goal area.
history.reasoning: Current selectable is fully boxed in by walls; switch control to the other selectable at [33, 21] to attempt reaching the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_UP | Try moving up into the adjacent goal area; goals are non-collidable so this may succeed even if the heuristic says blocked.
history.reasoning: Try moving up into the adjacent goal area; goals are non-collidable so this may succeed even if the heuristic says blocked.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_RIGHT | Try stepping into the adjacent goal area; the heuristic shows a wall too, but the goal itself is non-collidable so movement may still succeed.
history.reasoning: Try stepping into the adjacent goal area; the heuristic shows a wall too, but the goal itself is non-collidable so movement may still succeed.
